title: Brahma Ashtakam
intro: A sacred eight-verse hymn dedicated to Lord Brahma, the creator of the universe, recited for wisdom and intellectual growth.
description:
The Brahma Ashtakam is a powerful devotional composition consisting of eight verses (ashtakam) that praises the creative power of Lord Brahma. Each verse captures different aspects of the divine creator's magnificence and role in creation.

|| Verse 1 ||
Brahma Brahmajanaka Brahma Svarupah,
Brahma Nityam Brahma Sarvatmakah.
Brahma Sakshi Brahma Parama Brahma,
Brahma Devam Brahma Namami Satah.

This ashtakam is recited for blessings of knowledge, creativity, and divine understanding.
meaning: The Brahma Ashtakam celebrates Lord Brahma as the eternal creator and source of all knowledge. It seeks his blessings for wisdom, creativity, and spiritual enlightenment.

faq.a1: Ashtakam literally means 'eight verses'. Each verse is specifically crafted to capture different aspects of the deity.
faq.a2: Early mornings on Thursdays are considered auspicious, but can be recited any time.
faq.a3: Enhanced intellectual capacity, clarity of thought, and spiritual wisdom.
